title: Implementation summary key_files points at removed command path
evidence: Line 20 quotes stale key_files entry: `- ".opencode/commands/create/parent-skill.md"`. Actual command is now `.opencode/commands/create/sk-skill-parent.md`: `.opencode/commands/create/sk-skill-parent.md:7` quotes `# /create:sk-skill-parent Router` and line 45 quotes `canonical /create:sk-skill-parent entrypoint`. Glob confirmed only `.opencode/commands/create/sk-skill-parent.md` exists for `*parent*` command markdown.
why: This is a broken `key_files` path, not just stale prose; resume/review consumers following the packet metadata land on a non-existent file.
fix: Replace `.opencode/commands/create/parent-skill.md` with `.opencode/commands/create/sk-skill-parent.md` in the implementation-summary `_memory.continuity.key_files` block.

title: Implementation summary still names the old /create:parent-skill command
evidence: Line 3 quotes `the /create:parent-skill scaffolder`; line 58 quotes ``/create:parent-skill` scaffolder` and `commands/create/parent-skill.md`; line 100 quotes `The `/create:parent-skill` `update` branch`. Actual router says `/create:sk-skill-parent` at `.opencode/commands/create/sk-skill-parent.md:7` and line 45.
why: The shipped command was renamed after these docs were written, so packet completion docs now advertise the wrong operator entrypoint.
fix: Update these references to `/create:sk-skill-parent` and `commands/create/sk-skill-parent.md`, preserving only intentionally legacy asset filenames if they remain.

title: Parent spec says sk-doc section §21, but shipped section is §10
evidence: Line 72 quotes `sk-doc/references/skill_creation.md §21 + parent-skill hub/registry templates`; the same parent spec line 92 quotes `Formalize: sk-doc §10 + hub/registry templates`, and the actual shipped heading is `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/references/skill_creation.md:1018` `## 10. PARENT SKILLS WITH NESTED MODE PACKETS`.
why: This is doc staleness in the parent spec: one scope bullet points reviewers to a non-shipped section number while the completed phase and actual sk-doc file use §10.
fix: Change the parent spec line 72 reference from `§21` to `§10`.

title: Command rename left internal asset names on create_parent_skill_*
evidence: Line 7 quotes `# /create:sk-skill-parent Router`, while lines 15-17 route to `.opencode/commands/create/assets/create_parent_skill_presentation.txt`, `create_parent_skill_auto.yaml`, and `create_parent_skill_confirm.yaml`. The YAML assets themselves now declare `# Entrypoint: /create:sk-skill-parent` at `create_parent_skill_auto.yaml:5` and `create_parent_skill_confirm.yaml:5`.
why: This does not break routing because the router points at the existing assets, but it leaves an internal naming mismatch that will keep surfacing as stale-looking `parent_skill` references after the command was renamed to `sk-skill-parent`.
fix: Either rename the three assets to `create_sk_skill_parent_*` and update router/index references, or explicitly document that `create_parent_skill_*` is retained as an internal legacy asset stem.

title: mcp-open-design doctor does not check repo .utcp_config.json at all
evidence: Lines 75-95 enter "-- MCP config presence --" but only set `OPENCODE_CONFIG="$HOME/.config/opencode/opencode.json"`, grep that file for `open-design`, and run `claude mcp list ... | grep -qi 'open-design'`; there is no `UTCP="$HERE/../../../../.utcp_config.json"` or `.utcp_config.json` probe. The repo root `.utcp_config.json` exists and contains `"name": "open_design"` at line 127.
why: The three other checked doctors live at `.opencode/skills/<skill>/scripts/`, where `../../../../.utcp_config.json` resolves to the repo root correctly. Open Design lives at the same depth, but because it has no UTCP probe, it cannot report the existing Code Mode `open_design` manual and can mask a real registration as absent from its doctor output.
fix: Add the same repo-root UTCP probe in mcp-open-design doctor with `UTCP="$HERE/../../../../.utcp_config.json"`, guard it with `[ -f "$UTCP" ]` and `if grep ...; then ...; else info ...; fi`, and grep for the actual manual name `open_design`.
